title: Common 2-word phrases sprint
description: Frequent word pairs — train them as units
text: of the in the to the for the on the at the with the from the by the as the and the but the so the if the when the where the after the before the about the

tips.text: Short word pairs like these make up a tenth of ordinary writing. Drill them and whole phrases come for free.
Run the whole pair as one breath, no pause at the space. The hands should not stop to think mid-phrase.

tips.target: of the in the to the for the on the at the with the from the by the as

tips.text: When 'the' falls out of your hand without a thought, you have arrived. It is the most-typed word there is; make peace with it.

tips.target: the and the but the so the if the when the where the after the before the about the

character_tips.anna: Typing in phrases is where the real speed sits. Words stop being single things and become groups. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: Stop typing word by word. Type the phrase whole. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Words linked into one chain, fed out smooth. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: A phrase typed in one line, like a sentence read aloud. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
